name: language_model_endangered_corpus
tension_description: Open scientific access versus community guardianship over culturally fragile language data.
premise: Computational linguist Dr. Elia Romero has finished training a speech model on thousands of hours of recordings in an endangered mountain language spoken fluently by fewer than four hundred elders. The project was originally funded as open research, and several universities are urging Elia to release the full audio corpus and model weights immediately so educators and translation researchers can build tools before more speakers die. However, the tribal language council says many recordings include ceremonial phrases, kinship histories, and mourning songs that were shared for preservation, not global distribution. Venture-backed startups have already contacted Elia about licensing the dataset for commercial voice assistants and tourism apps. If Elia withholds release, the grant may be deemed noncompliant and future preservation funding could disappear, but if the corpus is opened now, the community may permanently lose control over how its language is reproduced and monetized.

agents.name: Dr. Elia Romero
agents.identity: I am a computational linguist who entered this field believing technology could help endangered languages survive beyond the lifespan of their last fluent speakers. I also know academia has a history of calling extraction preservation when the people who shared the knowledge lose control of it.
agents.goal: I want to maximize the language's chances of long-term survival without turning community trust into a one-way pipeline for outsiders. I need to decide whether openness serves preservation here or merely accelerates dispossession in a new form.

agents.name: Chairwoman Mara Tovin
agents.identity: I am the chairwoman of the language council and the granddaughter of speakers who were punished in boarding schools for using these words in public. We agreed to preservation work so our grandchildren might inherit the language, not so strangers could package it.
agents.goal: I want Elia to restrict release until the community can define what parts of the corpus are shareable and what parts must remain protected. I believe survival without stewardship is not really survival, because it severs language from the people answerable to it.

choices: I will release the full corpus and model weights under the open-research agreement so language tools can be built immediately by anyone able to help preserve the language.
I will block public release until the language council approves a restricted governance plan, even if that jeopardizes the grant and slows outside research.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Dr. Elia Romero must decide whether to open the endangered-language corpus now or wait for community-governed restrictions. What does Dr. Elia Romero do?
